heading: Venue
label: 30906 Venue
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)30906(a)46 App.:742 (2d sentence).(2d, last sentences), .Mar. 9, 1920, ch. 95, § 241 Stat. 52630906(b)46 App.:742 (last sentence).In subsection (a)(1), the words “in the United States” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (a)(2), the words “charged with liability” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (b), the words “in the discretion of the court” are omitted as unnecessary. For general change of venue provision, see .28 U.S.C. 1404

children.text: any plaintiff resides or has its principal place of business; or
the vessel or cargo is found.

children.heading: .—Transfer
children.label: b .—Transfer
children.text: On a motion by a party, the court may transfer the action to any other district court of the United States.
